comments.comment_text:
The schedule proposed by Geoview includes all stand-by due to  weather, typhoon and entanglement with fishermen. 
Please check the Contract and revise the wording to avoid confusion.
13/08/2025  : To avoid unnecessary confusion, suggest to update the wording per below: 
 "The Geoview schedule includes additional potential standby time due to weather (8 days), typhoons (3 days), and potential conflicts with fishing activities (5 days). However, if the actual standby time exceeds expectations, Geoview will request for a time extension. Then, Employer will review if Geoview's EoT requests are in accordance with Contract for review.

comments.comment_text:

25/08/2025 : As Geoview has already informed VTS/Navy with the survey plan/schedule, there will be no unexpected restrictions that could affect the survey schedule. In case VTS controls to site to avoid conflicts with incoming shipping vessels, Geoview to follow such instructions for safe operation. 

As shared, Geoview to include the statement "Navy's military training scheduled will be frequently checked in every 2 times/week and geophy plan will be established/updated. In case military training (shooting) is confirmed to affect the geophy schedule, Geoview will move to safe zone in accordance with VTS/Navy's control while the available geophy survey area will be checked in parallel to avoid any delays and the survey will be resumed for safe zones."

PEP has been revised, comment is closed.

We will correct 'daytime work' to 'working time'.
What we meant is that our current schedule does not take into account unexpected limitations on working time that may arise due to VTS (Vessel Traffic Service) controls, military exercises, rescue operations.

Geoview plans to supply food, fuel, and change the crew during weather standby periods to minimize the impact on the project schedule. However, if favorable weather continues for more than 15 days, we will request a port call.

Ultimately, Geoview shall meet the Annex 1.2 Time Schedule with or without port calls.

Geoview plans to supply food, fuel, and change the crew during weather standby periods to minimize the impact on the project schedule. However, if favorable weather continues for more than 15 days, we will request a port call. While this may affect the schedule, the impact is expected to be minimal 

22/08/2025  : 
We will apply as below

Geoview plans to supply food, fuel, and change the crew during weather standby periods to minimize the impact on the project schedule. However, if favorable weather continues for more than 15 days, we will request a port call and time extending.

Please understand that port calls may occur, even if the likelihood is low, and that they do require physical time to complete

25/08/2025
Will follow to your request, because we checked there is no fishing gear in site

comments.comment_text:
Let us know how Geoview will communicate with fishermen and be responsible for mobilisation of resources to minimise the potential impacts of fishing activities on site. There shall be personnel to be onboarded the vessels working on site to communicate with fishermen present on site. 
- Clause 11.2 of the Scope of Work clearly states that “the Contractor shall provide a Fisheries Liaison Officer (FLO) for the duration of the Works”. In addition, Clause 8.13 of the General Conditions places responsibility for on-site communication with fishermen squarely on the Contractor. We take the firm view that this obliges the Contractor to assign a separate and dedicated individual to the FLO role. The FLO function should not be combined with other operational duties and cannot be fulfilled on an ad hoc or part-time basis. 
Communication plan/orgainzation with fishermen will be proposed by Geoview and reviewed by Employer. 

13/08/2025  : Geoview to update the 3. Communication Plan by including FLO's details following Geoview's decision for sourcing. 
25/08/2025 : Geoview to provide response and update the 3. Communication Plan. 

07/09/2025:
Comment is closed
comments.summary_ko: Let us know how Geoview will communicate with fishermen and be responsible for m... 검토 및 수정 필요.
comments.response_text:
I fully understand, and Geoview is currently in negotiations to secure a suitable FLO. The candidate has a strong relationship with the fishermen near Anma Island and has prior experience working on the Anma Offshore Wind Farm. However, he is concerned that it may be difficult to gain cooperation from fishermen in other regions, as they are less likely to respond positively to such requests.

25/08/2025  :  Applied to PEP

comments.comment_text:
Guard Vessel / Scout vessel
Please share a rotation schedule for the three boats.
Since the guard vessel has a 3 days autonomy, what happens for the G-Ocean when guard vessel has to go back to port? Is there a risk in operating G-Ocean with no Guard vessel around? Should this be scheduled during daytime to ensure guard vessel support at night (higher operational risk)? Can the scouting vessel act as guard vessel in the meantime if needed?
13/08/2025  : Please update the detailed plan in the PEP for review.

By the way, what is the max. offshore endurance duration of guard vessel?  
07/09/2025:
Noted, comment is closed
comments.summary_ko: Guard Vessel / Scout vessel 기술적 확인/수정 필요.
comments.response_text:
The scout vessel will completed his mission and be demobilized before commence offshore campaign.
The guard vessel will complete to searche for fishing gear in the planned survey area for at least the next two days, after which it will make a port call.Meanwhile, the survey vessel will operate in the safest available area during the guard vessel’s absence.It will be reflected in PEP

04/09/2025  : The endurance duration is 3days, but she will operate continuously as much as possible under safety for catch up plan

comments.comment_text:
'This system will receive data in real-time from the Server Storage via SFPT'
Why is the 'in real time' barred?
13/08/2025  : Are we going to face the same limitation? In that case what is the expected data drop off schedule? Is is at port calls only? What is the impact on the overall processing and project schedule?

25/08/2025: Please address this comment
07/09/2025:
Noted. Comment closed
comments.summary_ko: 'This system will receive data in real-time from the Server Storage via SFPT' 검토 및 수정 필요.
comments.response_text:
In a recent project, the MOF did not allow data to be transferred from the vessel to the office via internet services.
As a result, Geoview has been transferring data to the office using secure portable storage devices and conducting QC tasks remotely on the onboard computer for the same reason.
04/09/2025  : It can be effected to schedule, but we will do as follwing method to reduce the effection
a. Delivery HDD to office by delivery service or hand carry
b. The data processing team in office will check data and make flow chart via remove control
c. QC person process data as much as we can on vessel

comments.comment_text:
Geoview to confrim the below: 
If the performance of the Services is disrupted or delayed because of identified fishing gears/nets/ropes/activities, the Contractor shall immediately notify the Employer without delay of the date of the occurrence of such delay and inform the Employer of the expected duration of the delay. The Contractor shall be responsible for proposing the best mitigation plan in order to manage the risk raised by the identified fishing gears/nets/ropes/activities and to progress the work as efficiently as possible. The mitigation plan shall be approved by the Client Representative on board.

13/08/2025  : Noted on your compliance in accordance with Contract. 

Your concern will be taken into consideration and shared with CRs as well.
comments.summary_ko: Geoview to confrim the below: 기술적 확인/수정 필요.
comments.response_text:
We follow this condition of contract.

Geoview will diligently follow all relevant procedures and draw upon its experience and expertise to deliver the best possible results.
However, fishing nets and strong currents in the West Sea may pose safety risks and affect the quality of the acquired data. We kindly ask you to be fully aware of these potential challenges.
Without above understand of potential risk, there have been instances where accidents—such as fishing net damage and equipment loss—occurred because CRs or managers prioritized quality and progress over safety, and insist to survey, even under hazardous condition

comments.comment_text:
Geoview to confirm the following: 
The Contractor shall be responsible for mobilisation of resources to minimise the potential impacts of fishing activities on site. This includes but is not limited to:
i. scouting survey to identify fishing gears/nets/ropes/activities present on or in the vicinity of the Site one week before the start of the mobilization; and
ii: mobilisation and management of guard vessel(s) which shall operate based on a 24hours/day in order to identify fishing gears/nets/ropes/activities present on or in the vicinity of the route of the survey vessel in advance of the passage of the survey vessel. Whenever fishing gears/nets/ropes/activities are identified, in accordance with Article 8.1.5 "Contractor to inform the Employer", the Contractor shall immediately notify the Employer without delay and propose the best mitigation plan in order to manage the risk raised by the identified fishing gears/nets/ropes/activities and to progress the work as efficiently as possible. The mitigation plan shall be approved by the Client Representative on board; and
iii. mobilisation and management of personnel on board the vessels working on Site in order to communicate with fishermen present on site.
13/08/2025  : Same as item 34.
comments.summary_ko: Geoview to confirm the following: 기술적 확인/수정 필요.
comments.response_text: Same with item 34

comments.comment_text:
'This potential gap in coverage will be discussed with the CR on site, and if necessary, an infill survey or other mitigation measures will be considered.'

Potential mitigation could be to not account for some of the line length in the blue area and perform lines along the military order winthin the blue area. This would prevent adding extra line length to the survey. Thus can be further discussed during clarificatons and with CR during OPS.
13/08/2025  : Understood
07/09/2025:
Comment is closed
comments.summary_ko: 'This potential gap in coverage will be discussed with the CR on site 검토 및 수정 필요.

comments.comment_text:
Suitable adjustments and/or revisions shall be required in areas where fishing activity or the presence of fishing nets or buoys is identified. This includes rescheduling the work, adding in-fill lines when possible, or avoiding areas which are deemed unsafe for work.

Any change or reduction of survey coverage or line plan shall only be considered as a last resort, and Geoview shall take all necessary steps to avoid any impact on the quality or quantity of survey.

Geoview shall be also responsible for proactively identifying site conditions through advance scouting surveys and the operation of a guard vessel, and for preparing appropirate mitigation plans in advance. The objective is to minimize the need for multiple or complex mitigation proposals requiring onbaord approval from teh Employer's Representative during operations.

Geoview shall not be excused from any failure to acquire data within the intended survey areas unless it has: (i) proactively identified site conditions through advance scouting surveys and the operation of a guard vessel; (ii) immediately notified the Employer of the obstruction and the expected duration of the delay;and (iii) proposed the best available mitigation plan to manage the obstruction and to progress the work as efficiently as possible; and (iv) obtained prior approval of such mitigation plan from the Employer’s onboard representative, pursuant to Clause 8.1.5 of the Contract.

This exemption shall not apply in cases where the failure is attributable to Geoview’s fault or negligence.
13/08/2025  :  Noted on your compliance in accordance with Contract.

It is minimu presurvey lines. if possible, survey lines will be incleased.
Instead of aiming to obtain complete information during the presurvey, the goal is to provide a rough estimate of fishing net locations. Based on this preliminary data, the guard vessel will conduct a more detailed inspection.

GNSS service failures are extremely rare. However, in the event of a primary device failure, a secondary device with easy identical settings can be used as a backup.

comments.response_text:
The term "unstable sediments" refers to shallow subsurface layers that may pose geotechnical risks due to low bearing capacity. These include soft clay layers, potentially liquefiable sandy layers, and strata sensitive to repeated loading.

comments.response_text:
Added your comment to PEP
In UHRS interpretation, key structural features such as faults and folds are identified and analyzed in terms of their orientation, scale, and displacement characteristics. Faults are typically recognized by discontinuities in reflection patterns or abrupt amplitude changes, while folds are interpreted based on curvature and repetitive reflection sequences.
22/08/2025  :
Has been applied to PEP

comments.comment_text:
For produced maps and grids (Kingdom, ArcGis), please use the folowing color palette:
https://research.google/blog/turbo-an-improved-rainbow-colormap-for-visualization/
The associated kingdom file can be provided.
For the coloring, please follow the below rules:
 - For elevation maps, use warm colors for shallow, cold for deep;
 - For thickness maps, use warm for thick, cold for thin;
 - Round displayed values on maps at multiples of 10 of 5, no decimals.
13/08/2025  : ok- will provide (lyrx & clb).
For kingdom, a set of discrete versions of the color map are also sent. On arcgis discrete symbology can easily be set.
For how to use these colors in maps on reports, arcgis and kingdom:
I would like to have:
 - when displaying the depth of an horizon or the seabed: use the warm colors (red end) for the shallow values and the cold colors (blue end) for the deep values
 - when displaying the thickness of a stratigraphic layer, use the warm colors for the important values of thickness and the cold values for the thin values
 - last point is regarding the boundaries of the values that are displayed in a map. I would like them to be set at round values and at easily understandable limits (typically when showing the bathymetry at the site, say between 50 and 100m and with no decimals in the legend). This makes it easier to read and understand the information displayed on a map when going through a report.

25/08/2025: lyr and clb files were sent on the 08/08/2025, please confirm reception and validity of files

08/09/2025: in the favorite styles file shared for ArcGis, you will find the preferred following palette for rasters:

Noted that the MBES and SBP transducers were installed in the same location, which will most likely cause interference (noise) in the MBES data. comments.comment_text:
The operational diagram of the towing arrangement indicates that the streamer is deployed directly behind the stern, which will likely result in propeller noise being recorded during the survey. It is recommended to use a boom arm to increase the distance between the propeller and the streamer, thereby reducing noise levels and improving data quality.
12/09/2025:
Based on the sea trial result, the noise from the propeller was minimum and not affacted the data quality. Comments is closed.
